title: Buchnera arizonica
taxonomy: Buchnera arizonica is classified as a species of bacteria in the genus Buchnera, which belongs to the family Bartonellaceae. This family is within the class Alphaproteobacteria, in the phylum Proteobacteria. The kingdom to which it belongs is Bacteria. Buchnera arizonica is a symbiotic bacterium found in certain insects, specifically leafhoppers. It plays a crucial role in the nutrition of its host insect by providing essential amino acids.
